User Story: As an API client I want to run the full user+book lifecycle so I can trust the API end to end
Scenario: Complete user book lifecycle (provision, authorize, browse, build & mutate collection, tear down)
Preconditions: DemoQA API reachable; catalog has >= 2 books
Test Steps: 1. Register a fresh user 2. Generate token 3. POST /Account/v1/Authorized with credentials 4. GET user (empty collection) 5. GET /BookStore/v1/Books and capture first two ISBNs 6. Add first ISBN 7. Verify user owns it 8. PUT replace with second ISBN 9. Verify replacement 10. DELETE single book 11. Verify empty 12. DELETE user 13. GET deleted user
Expected Result: Authorized returns 'true'; user starts empty; add returns 201 with the ISBN; replace returns 200 with new ISBN and without old; delete book -> 204 and empty collection; delete user -> 204; subsequent GET user -> 401

User Story: As a new user I want my profile to reflect my identity and an empty library
Scenario: A newly created user has the expected identity and an empty collection
Preconditions: Fresh user provisioned via auth.feature; valid Bearer token
Expected Result: Status 200; response.userId == userId; response.username == username; response.books == []

User Story: As a user I want valid credentials to be reported as authorized
Scenario: Valid credentials are reported as authorized
Expected Result: Status 200; response body is the literal 'true'

User Story: As a user I want a deleted account to become inaccessible
Scenario: A deleted user can no longer be retrieved
Preconditions: Fresh user provisioned; valid Bearer token

User Story: As a browser I want a valid, non-empty catalog so I can pick books
Scenario: The catalog returns a schema-valid, non-empty list of books
Expected Result: Status 200; response matches all-books schema; response.books.length > 0

User Story: As a browser I want to open a single book and see consistent details
Scenario: A single book is retrievable by ISBN and matches the catalog entry
Expected Result: Both 200; single book matches book schema; isbn, title and author match the catalog entry

User Story: As a browser I want an unknown ISBN to be rejected clearly
Scenario: Requesting a non-existent ISBN is rejected

User Story: As a user I want a book I add to appear in my collection
Scenario: A book added to the collection is owned by the user
Preconditions: Fresh user + token; catalog has >= 2 books; selectedIsbn/replacementIsbn from live catalog
Expected Result: Add returns 201 and books contain selectedIsbn; GET user shows selectedIsbn in books

User Story: As a user I want to replace one book with another
Scenario: A book in the collection can be replaced with another
Preconditions: Fresh user + token; book already added
Test Steps: 1. Add selectedIsbn 2. PUT /BookStore/v1/Books/{selectedIsbn} with replacementIsbn 3. GET user
Expected Result: Replace returns 200; books contain replacementIsbn; GET user contains replacementIsbn and no longer selectedIsbn

User Story: As a user I want to remove one book from my collection
Scenario: A single book can be removed from the collection
Preconditions: Fresh user + token; book already added
Test Steps: 1. Add selectedIsbn 2. DELETE /BookStore/v1/Book with {isbn, userId} 3. GET user
Expected Result: Delete returns 204; GET user shows books == []

User Story: As a user I want to clear my entire collection at once
Scenario: The entire collection can be cleared for a user
Preconditions: Fresh user + token; two books added
Test Steps: 1. Add selectedIsbn and replacementIsbn 2. DELETE /BookStore/v1/Books?UserId={userId} 3. GET user
Expected Result: Delete-all returns 204; GET user shows books == []

User Story: As a user I want duplicate additions to be rejected
Scenario: Adding a book already in the collection is rejected
Preconditions: Fresh user + token; book already added
Test Steps: 1. Add selectedIsbn (201) 2. Add the same selectedIsbn again
Expected Result: Second add returns 400; response.message contains 'ISBN already present'

User Story: As the API I must reject invalid credentials on token generation
Scenario: Token generation reports failure for invalid credentials
Test Steps: 1. POST /Account/v1/GenerateToken with a non-existent user + wrong password
Expected Result: Status 200; response.status == 'Failed'; result == 'User authorization failed.'; token == null

User Story: As the API I must report unknown users on the authorization check
Scenario: Authorization check fails for an unknown user

User Story: As the API I must reject duplicate registrations
Scenario: Registering an existing username is rejected
Preconditions: fixtureUser already exists on the environment

User Story: As the API I must reject registrations missing required fields
Scenario: Registration without a password is rejected

User Story: As the API I must block unauthenticated profile access
Scenario: Retrieving a user without a token is unauthorized
Preconditions: A random/fake userId; no Authorization header

User Story: As the API I must reject invalid tokens
Scenario: Retrieving a user with an invalid token is unauthorized

User Story: As the API I must block unauthenticated collection mutation
Scenario: Adding a book without a token is unauthorized
Preconditions: A random/fake userId; no Authorization header
Test Steps: 1. POST /BookStore/v1/Books with {userId, collectionOfIsbns:[{isbn}]} and no token
